qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Fully playable with movement, shooting, aliens, shields, waves, pause and restart, but visuals are simplified and gameplay lacks some classic behaviors like player death reset/invader animation and can feel a bit rough.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: Excellent implementation featuring destructible pixel-based shields, mystery ships, and a smooth difficulty curve as aliens are destroyed.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core mechanics are well-implemented with alien grid movement, destructible shields, mystery ship, and multiple waves, though aliens are drawn as simple colored rectangles rather than iconic pixel sprites, and the lives display overlaps with the lives count text.

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable and feature-complete with movement, shooting, shields, UFO, waves, pause and restart, but visuals are very simplified versus classic Space Invaders and there are some design/logic quirks such as odd player hitbox code, instant respawn after hit, and potentially awkward one-shot space handling.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally well-implemented Space Invaders clone featuring accurate alien movement, destructible pixel-grid shields, UFO logic, and a dynamic audio heartbeat.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core mechanics work well with alien formation movement, shooting, shields, UFO, and wave progression, though player collision box calculation has a redundant expression and shield arch shape is non-standard; visuals use colored rectangles rather than pixel-art sprites but layout is reasonable.
